lessons.title: Hand Rankings
lessons.description: Learn how poker hands rank from strongest to weakest.

questions.question: Which hand is strongest?
questions.explanation: A Full House beats a Straight and a Flush in standard poker rankings.

questions.question: What beats a Full House?
questions.explanation: Only Four of a Kind, Straight Flush, and Royal Flush beat a Full House.

questions.question: Which hand is weaker than a Flush?
questions.explanation: A Straight is below a Flush in hand ranking order.

questions.question: How many cards are in a poker hand?
questions.explanation: The best 5-card combination determines your poker hand.

questions.question: Which hand is strongest overall?
questions.explanation: A Royal Flush is the highest possible hand in poker.

lessons.title: Positions
lessons.description: Understand table position and why acting later is powerful.

questions.question: Which position acts last postflop?
questions.explanation: The Button has the best position because it acts last after the flop.

questions.question: Why is late position valuable?

answers.text: You get more information before acting
You can see opponents' hole cards

questions.explanation: Acting later gives you information from opponents' actions first.

questions.question: Which position is considered early?
questions.explanation: Under the Gun acts first preflop and is an early position.

questions.question: In which position should you play tighter ranges?
questions.explanation: Early position requires stronger starting hands due to acting first.

questions.question: Which seat is directly right of the Button?
questions.explanation: The Small Blind sits immediately to the right of the Button.

lessons.title: Preflop Decisions
lessons.description: Practice when to fold, call, and raise before the flop.

questions.question: With pocket aces preflop, your default play is usually:
questions.explanation: Pocket aces are premium and typically should be raised preflop.

questions.question: From early position, weak offsuit hands are usually:
questions.explanation: In early position, folding weak offsuit hands is generally best.

questions.question: What is a 3-bet?
questions.explanation: A 3-bet is the first re-raise in a betting sequence.

questions.question: Which starting hand is generally strongest?
questions.explanation: Pocket aces are the strongest preflop starting hand in Hold'em.

questions.question: In late position with a decent hand and folded action, you should often:
questions.explanation: Late position allows wider opens because fewer players remain to act.
